beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, grab work clothes.
beats.reasoning: Derek's routine: up at 5:30 AM no matter the weather. Yesterday's low energy (30/100) suggests he'll keep this morning tight and focused—just coffee and go.

beats.activity: Drive to Bellevue site in light rain, listening to sports radio.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon, so Derek leaves early (6am) to beat the closure and avoid the detour. Rain is steady by 7am, so he'll take I-405 south and loop around—adds 5 min but safer than gambling on 520.

beats.activity: Breakfast at diner near site, check crew messages.
beats.reasoning: Derek's standard: grab breakfast before 7am. Yesterday he felt guilty skipping to watch the match; today he's back to routine, no distractions. Rain outside, warm diner inside—routine anchors a low-energy day.

beats.activity: Concrete pour and framing setup, rain-adjusted work schedule.
beats.reasoning: Rain from 7am–2pm means Derek pivots to indoor framing and prep rather than outdoor concrete; keeps crew safe and productive. No World Cup distraction today—focus on the job. Works straight through to noon.

beats.activity: Lunch at site (sandwich, coffee inside due to rain).
beats.reasoning: Drizzle and rain all day means no porch break like yesterday. Derek eats quickly inside the trailer, still monitoring the 520 closure status for the drive home.

beats.activity: Afternoon framing inspection, crew coordination, daily log.
beats.reasoning: Rain tapers by 2pm, but Derek stays focused on the job. SR-520 closure lifted at noon, so traffic thins out. He wraps by 4:30pm to head home before the evening rush.

beats.activity: Drive home to Renton, I-405 south, light traffic post-closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure lifted at noon, so Derek takes the normal I-405 route south. Rain clearing, so a straightforward 50-min drive home.

beats.activity: Help kids with homework, prep dinner.
beats.reasoning: Derek's back by 5:20pm. Spends time with family—homework, kitchen prep. Low energy yesterday means he's not coaching tonight; just a quiet Tuesday at home.
Family dinner—same quiet rhythm as yesterday, but no porch break afterward because of the lingering drizzle and earlier rain.

beats.activity: Watch sports highlights, catch up on emails, wind down.
beats.reasoning: Derek unwinds indoors—TV, phone, maybe a beer. No outdoor sitting tonight. Low energy carries over; he's content to stay in, recharge for Wednesday.
Derek heads to bed by 10:30pm for a full 7-hour sleep before his 5:30am wake-up Wednesday. Solid recovery night after a disciplined rain day.

diary: Woke up early like always, dark and drizzly outside. The rain followed me all the way to Bellevue—grabbed coffee at Mel's before heading to the site, scrolling through crew messages between bites of eggs. The concrete pour went ahead despite the weather; we just adjusted the timeline and kept moving. Spent most of the morning and afternoon on framing inspections, checking measurements in the rain, making sure nothing got sloppy. The bridge closure had everyone scrambling but didn't hit us too hard. Drove home on lighter-than-expected 405 traffic, which was a gift. The kids needed help with math homework, which I'm still not great at, then we all sat down for dinner. Ended the night watching replays and catching up on emails that piled up. Tired but satisfied—another solid day on the job.
thought_process: The rain set the tone early, so I knew we'd need to stay flexible with the pour schedule. That bridge closure had me a little anxious about commute chaos, but by afternoon the I-405 was clear. I focused on what I could control: keeping the crew safe and on track, double-checking the framing work so nothing falls through the cracks. By evening I was honestly just ready to be home and disconnect. The homework help reminded me I should probably brush up on my geometry. Mood-wise, steady and grounded—nothing dramatic, just the rhythms of work and family keeping me centered.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
